programmes.show_description: For Love nor Money: Rising star Colin Buchanan is one of the hard-nosed cops. A retired detective is found dead on his local golf course, and Pascoe uncovers a tale of bribery.
Thirty Something: Sibling sitcom and the Essex girls have the birthday blues. Sharon is approaching her 30th birthday but is unable to see a bright future ahead of her.
A Morning Song: Five Points falls into a state of panic as a community safe haven is seized by one of the deadliest men in New York.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Love on the Run: More Essex comedy from the Chigwell sisters. Darryl has been taken to hospital with appendicitis and plans to escape and spend the night with his wife.
Father's Day: Boyden's estranged daughter asks him for protection. Boyden helps her out and they start to rekindle their relationship.
New. Tuning Out: Vivien is back to work at the Mill, while Joe's daughter Emily makes a visit from America to stay with her father and Daniel.
New. School's Out: George and Ronnie attend marriage counselling, and Nick faces a new challenge. An attack on a schoolboy forces his teacher to make a tough decision.
The Heart of the Matter: A heart becomes available, so Ed tries to ensure his father is the recipient by lying about the results of his blood test. Diane wants to know where she stands.
What Tonight Means to Me Part 1: A power cut plunges Holby into darkness just as Lenny prepares to accept the Jafa fellowship. Jordan discovers Ludlow's reason for the drug trial.
Father's Day: Boyden's estranged daughter asks him for protection. Boyden helps her out and they start to rekindle their relationship.
New. As Dr Al bids farewell to the Square, a familiar face returns. Becca has a cunning plan to get Stacey's stall back, and Tiff finds herself in danger. Is the game up for Syed?
New. Jack is stunned by a new piece of evidence - has he found Archie's murderer? Meanwhile, Syed is pressured by Zainab, and Bianca welcomes her little brother back to the Square.
The Bull with the Bowler Hat: Fun with the animal experts working in Yorkshire. While Tristan teaches artificial insemination, Siegfried only succeeds in enraging a bull.
Jan meets an interesting man at a fashion show in Rome. Tom and Sir Edward come to blows, and Kate persuades Jack to take up gambling again.
In My Memory Lock'd: Frank and Lu struggle to unlock the memories of an amnesiac with a mysterious connection to an exclusive hotel where no one recognises him.
A Test of Character: The immortal prison-set sitcom. When Godber studies to get another O-level before leaving jail, Fletch cannot understand his appetite for all the hard work.
Hermione (The Short Course): Young-at-heart sitcom. Howard is alarmed that his love life may feature in Clegg's memoirs. Smiler is aghast when a potential relationship suddenly ends.
Who's That Mouse in the Poetry Group?: Roy Clarke's record breaking sitcom. Smiler pretends to be an intellectual in an attempt to enchant Marina. Howard wants to keep an eye on them.
Available for Weddings: The world's longest-running sitcom! Cycling down a hillside, Clegg ends up with a fractured foot. Truly demonstrates how the accident could have been avoided.
New. Death by Vinyl: First Chapter: Members of Cat's old band reunite to record an album at a retreat not far from Wildermarsh - and it's not long before one of them turns up dead.
New. Full Moon Fever: A terrible night shift leaves the station under investigation. Grace doubts her career choice when Cal is arrested for resisting a racially discriminatory stop and search.
Another gripping case for our detectives using 1970s methods to combat crimes. The team re-investigate a bungled murder case where the body of a young girl was misidentified.
In My Memory Lock'd: Frank and Lu struggle to unlock the memories of an amnesiac with a mysterious connection to an exclusive hotel where no one recognises him.
Great Escapes: A spooky adventure for our heroes. Dalziel and Pascoe's attempts to recapture an escaped murderer lead them to face the mythical Barguest, a monstrous black dog.
Love on the Run: More Essex comedy from the Chigwell sisters. Darryl has been taken to hospital with appendicitis and plans to escape and spend the night with his wife.
To One Shortly to Die: A murder demands that Corcoran work closely with Brendan Donovan, who is eager to close the case. Robert Morehouse makes a difficult decision.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Old Friends: Sitcom with those Essex sisters. Tracey invites an old schoolfriend to stay in the guest room. As the pair relive their past adventures, Sharon begins to feel deserted.
Sweet Sixteen: Skase feels he is the laughing stock of CID when he is abducted and robbed by a gang of teenage girls.
New. Words Left Unsaid: Joe gets embroiled in a feud involving three sisters when he is called out to look after a dying patient.
New. Speak Up: Daniel makes a confession that appals Julia, and Michelle discovers her old English teacher sleeping rough behind the Mill.
Baptism of Fire: Two new nurses struggle to make a good impression on their first day as one of them questions Mubbs's judgement, while the other knocks Diane for six.
What Tonight Means to Me Part 2: Lenny and Yuki put aside their disagreement over the Jafa fellowship to deliver Shona's baby, and Jordan discovers Zoe is not pregnant.
Sweet Sixteen: Skase feels he is the laughing stock of CID when he is abducted and robbed by a gang of teenage girls.
New. As Marsden resolves to discover the identity of Archie's murderer, Jack turns up the pressure on Bradley. Bianca's siblings make sure that her hen night is one to remember.
New. As police rumours circle the Square, will Ronnie be able to follow through with her promise of a fresh start? Max does what he can to protect Bradley.
The Pig Man Cometh: Cracking vintage drama with the Yorkshire vets tackling different dilemmas each time. When James diagnoses a case of swine fever, a freeholder may be ruined.
Jack goes for a drink with an old friend from his wartime days, and the pair discuss an exciting business venture. Sir Edward makes one final pass at Jan.
The Envious Court: Frank and Lu investigate a death threat at Runningbrook, an exclusive tennis club which hides a toxic secret.
Final Stretch: Another of Ronnie Barker's array of classic comedy characters. The inimitable Fletch has to help Lennie out after he gets involved in a fight a week before his parole.
The McDonaghs of Jamieson Street: Young-at-heart sitcom about a group of retired friends. Truly, Alvin and Billy set out to track down a woman who has taken a shine to Smiler.
The Afterthoughts of a Co-op Manager: Enduringly popular sitcom. Marina resolves to give up Howard out of respect for a lost love who's supposedly moved to Australia to forget her.
Lot No. 8: The world's longest-running sitcom! Overcome by the lure of a bargain, Auntie Wainwright makes a winning bid at the auction rooms, even though she doesn't know what it is.
The Tanganyika Green: GK Chesterton's sleuthing padre gets involved once again to investigate the murder of a Colonial Postal Service officer, when the fair comes to Kembleford.
The first episode of the acclaimed British drama series about a Yorkshire police sergeant whose world stops when the man who destroyed her family is released from prison.
Another adventure for our eccentric bunch of middle-aged detectives. The team are approached by a tabloid newspaper editor, who claims a celebrity chef murdered her husband.
The Envious Court: Frank and Lu investigate a death threat at Runningbrook, an exclusive tennis club which hides a toxic secret.
